name: Langley Paper Mills

surface_od_ft.qualifier: as written 'ca 190·00 ... O.D.' — 'ca 190' clear, trailing figures read '·00'

units: fathoms/feet/inches, transcribed verbatim — convert ONLY downstream

annotations: depth cell printed '7 0, 0' — stray comma/ink dot; reads 7-0-0
'metal' = argillaceous shale/mudstone; sits within the superficial-to-bedrock passage of this shallow hole
'rotten' = decomposed/weathered; directly overlies the coal; clerk used 'stone' (not 'post'=sandstone nor 'metal'=shale) so sandstone-vs-mudstone is genuinely ambiguous
typed seam name beside COAL: 'Low Main Swam' (Seam — clerk typo 'Swam' for 'Seam')
handwritten ink marginalia: 'Brass Thill' — a later correlation note (Brass Thill is a separate named Durham seam)
'hard' = competent bed; generic 'stone' — sandstone-vs-mudstone ambiguous; bottom of hole

seam_marks.how: named in original: typed 'Low Main Swam' (Seam) beside COAL
handwritten (ink) marginalia beside COAL — later BGS correlation note

notes: Header: 'SECTION OF borehole at Langley Paper Mills'; BGS index lists name as 'LNAGLEY PAPER MILLS' (apparent index typo). NGR NZ 2575 4068; Communicated by 'B & S. 1273'; One-inch Map 26, Six-inch Map Durham 26 NE. BGS registration stamp 'NZ 24 SE 21 PAGE NO. 1'. Date of boring/Borer blank; year null (the form's printed code '(68/4028) Wt.74179/81 3000 2/49' is a stationery date, not the boring date). All ten depths reconcile exactly with running thickness sums — no clerk arithmetic slips. Final depth 17 fathoms 2 ft 4 in = 104.333 ft = 31.80 m (near index 31.71). Lithology terms are pit-sinker vernacular: 'metal'=shale, 'ramble'=roof shale, 'stone'/'rotten stone'/'hard stone' left as sandstone (probable) because the clerk avoided both 'post' and 'metal'. A faint pencil scrawl (reads like '128'/'l28') sits in the blank area well below the table — not a seam correlation, not transcribed as an interval. Page 2 is a continuation sheet showing only the printed column header (Thickness / Depth from Surface) with no strata — no log content.
